overview: This deck leverages land drops and mana acceleration to rapidly deploy Omnath, Locus of Creation, generating massive card advantage and mana advantage. Solitude and Teferi, Time Raveler provide board control and tempo while the engine ramps into an insurmountable resource lead. The goal is to outvalue opponents through relentless land plays and protective permanents.
win_conditions: Outvalue opponents with Omnath's land-drop counters and mana generation
Stall the board with Solitude and Teferi, Time Raveler until resources are exhausted
Deploy a wide board of lands and creatures to overwhelm defenses

keep_criteria: Hands with 2+ lands and a cheap spell or creature
Hands containing Omnath, Locus of Creation with early land drops
Hands with Solitude or Teferi, Time Raveler and sufficient mana
Keep hands with 2-3 lands (including at least one fetch/shock or equivalent fixing), a turn-1 land drop, and either Teferi, Time Raveler, Time Raveler, or a cheap interaction/removal spell.
Prioritize hands that can play a land and cast a 1-2 mana interaction or draw spell on turn 1 or 2.
mulligan_criteria: Hands with only high-cost permanents and no acceleration
Hands lacking interaction or early plays
Mulligan hands with 0-1 lands, unfixable multi-color demands, or only high-cost spells without early answers.

early_game: Prioritize land drops and cheap mana rocks to accelerate into Omnath
Play Solitude early to disrupt opponent's board development
Use Teferi, Time Raveler to protect key permanents and gain tempo
Turn 1: Play a land, cast Teferi, Time Raveler or Time Raveler if mana allows.
Turn 2: Develop mana, cast a counterspell or removal, or play a second land.
Against aggro/combo, prioritize interaction over ramp.
Against control/ramp, develop mana and draw cards.
Sequence lands to ensure W/U/R/G availability by turn 3-4 for Omnath, holding up blue mana for protection when possible.
mid_game: Focus on playing lands to trigger Omnath's counters and generate mana
Use generated mana to cast additional threats or answer opponent's plays
Maintain board presence with Solitude and Teferi while ramping
Hoard mana for Omnath (typically 5-6 mana) and protection spells.
Use card draw to maintain hand size and track opponent resources.
Treat the graveyard as a secondary concern unless facing recursion engines
prioritize keeping Solitude and Teferi, Time Raveler in play as they provide both protection and removal.
Never overextend mana into a single threat without backup interaction.
Commit threats only when you have interaction or a board wipe ready.
late_game: Leverage massive mana advantage to deploy multiple threats simultaneously
Use Omnath's counters to draw cards or generate additional resources
Close out the game with an unblockable board state or direct damage
Omnath, Locus of Creation is the primary finisher.
Once it resolves and enters, follow up with token generation, card draw, and protection.
Solitude can exile opponent's removal or threats to secure the win.
Teferi, Time Raveler can lock down the board and enable Omnath.
Win by out-valuing the opponent through card advantage and board presence.
Go all-in when you have resolved Omnath, have protection (Solitude or counters), and the opponent lacks immediate removal or combo answers.

key_cards.name: Omnath, Locus of Creation
key_cards.role: Core engine that generates mana and counters when lands are played
key_cards.notes: Prioritize playing lands to maximize its triggers; use counters to draw cards or generate additional mana

key_cards.name: Solitude
key_cards.notes: Play early to prevent opponent from developing their board; use its ability to bounce threats or protect your permanents

key_cards.name: Teferi, Time Raveler
key_cards.notes: Use to bounce opponent's threats, protect Omnath, or draw cards; keep it on the battlefield as long as possible

sequencing_tips: Always play lands before other spells to trigger Omnath's abilities
Hold up mana for Solitude's ability to bounce key threats
Use Teferi, Time Raveler's abilities to respond to opponent's plays rather than proactively
Prioritize keeping Solitude and Teferi on the battlefield to maintain tempo
Common mistakes include overcommitting to Omnath without protection, mismanaging mana colors, ignoring opponent's graveyard, and failing to track opponent's hand size.
Unique interactions: Solitude can exile permanents from any zone, making it versatile for protection and removal.
Teferi, Time Raveler's loyalty counters can be a resource.
Always be mindful of the legendary rule if you control multiple Omnaths.
Practice timing your protection spells to counter opponent's removal or combo finishers.

matchups.advice: Play Solitude early to disrupt their board and buy time for Omnath to come online
watch_for: Board wipes that clear your Solitude

matchups.advice: Ramp quickly to outvalue them and use Teferi to protect your permanents from their removal
Balance land drops with threats to pressure their board while ramping into Omnath

Use split second (if available), cheap interaction, or resolve threats through layers. Play around counters by having backup plans or using protection. If possible, resolve Omnath when the opponent has no mana up. Force them to use resources on you before committing your win condition.

Out-grind them with card draw and removal. Use Teferi, Time Raveler to slow their mana development. Prioritize removing their ramp sources and finishers. Don't trade up unnecessarily; wait for them to overextend or make a mistake, then capitalize with your engine.

Win the resource war. Prioritize keeping interaction and counters. Use Solitude to exile opponent's Omnath or Teferi, Time Raveler. Win the card advantage battle by drawing more and protecting your own threats. Track their hand size and mana to time your plays perfectly.

common_threats: Watch for opponent's mana development (ramp vs. control), hand size (combo decks often go all-in), and graveyard activity (recursion engines).
Key tells: Opponent tapping for multiple colors early suggests ramp/combo
holding up blue mana suggests counterspells
tapping for black/red suggests targeted removal or discard.
Track when they tap out for their win condition to time your protection spells.
